title: TechCrunch Expands to Tokyo with SusHi Tech 2026 Focused on AI and Robotics
summary: TechCrunch is bringing its Startup Battlefield to Tokyo for SusHi Tech 2026, spotlighting AI, robotics, and other transformative technologies. The event will feature live demos and discussions on how these innovations are reshaping industries.
body:
TechCrunch is set to host its first major event in Tokyo, Japan, with SusHi Tech 2026. The conference will focus on four key technology domains: AI, robotics, resilience, and entertainment. This expansion marks a significant step for TechCrunch, bringing its Startup Battlefield competition to a new global market.

The event will feature live demos of humanoid robots, sessions on the software revolution in autonomous driving, and deep dives into cyber defense and climate tech. Additionally, there will be candid conversations about how AI is transforming the global music and anime industries. This broad range of topics underscores the event's aim to explore the cutting-edge technologies that are reshaping society.

Reactions to the announcement have been positive, with industry experts praising TechCrunch's move to tap into Tokyo's vibrant tech scene. The event is expected to attract a diverse array of startups, investors, and tech enthusiasts. As AI and robotics continue to advance, SusHi Tech 2026 could set the stage for new collaborations and innovations in these fields.
twitterThread:
🚀 TechCrunch is bringing the Startup Battlefield to Tokyo! 🗼

AI, robotics, and more take center stage at SusHi Tech 2026.
Here's the lineup: live humanoid robot demos, autonomous driving software deep dives, and cyber defense talks.
The wild part? They're also exploring how AI is rewriting music and anime. Yes, anime.
Let that sink in. TechCrunch is doubling down on Tokyo's tech scene. This changes everything for global startups.
Hot take: This could be the event that puts Tokyo on the map as a top tech hub. Watch this space.
